mnemonics: CIA to TCP: Confidentiality (C), Integrity (I), and Availability (A) are the core security objectives that Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) and others are designed to help achieve.
PROtocols SECure Objectives (PRO SEC O): A protocol is built to SECure the core security Objectives.
keyPoints: Security begins with defining clear objectives, primarily Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA).
A security protocol is a formal set of rules and messages designed to achieve one or more of these security objectives.
The design process involves translating abstract objectives into concrete cryptographic mechanisms (e.g., encryption for confidentiality, hashing for integrity).
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of different protocols (e.g., using SSL/TLS for integrity when it is primarily for confidentiality and authentication).
Assuming a single protocol can achieve all security objectives simultaneously without careful design and combination.
Overlooking non-technical aspects, such as policy and user training, which are also necessary to fully meet security objectives.
